Time to the outcome event was defined as days from participant specific follow-up start date (Supplement, Table S1).”[...]“Demographic variables included age, sex, obesity, ethnicity, region and deprivation.”[...]“Age was modelled using a restricted cubic spline…”

code_text:
surv_formula_age_spl_sex <- paste0(
  "Surv(lcovid_surv, lcovid_cens) ~ ",
  "cov_cat_sex",
  "+rms::rcs(cov_num_age,parms=knot_placement)"
)
[...]cov_factor_names <- names(input)[grepl("cov_cat", names(input))]
cov_num_names <- names(input)[grepl("cov_num", names(input))]
if(analysis != "infected"){
  cov_factor_names <- cov_factor_names[!cov_factor_names %in% c("cov_cat_sex","cov_cat_ie.status",
                                                              "cov_cat_covid_phenotype")] # remove sex and vaccination status
}else{
  cov_factor_names <- cov_factor_names[!cov_factor_names %in% c("cov_cat_sex","cov_cat_ie.status")] # remove sex and vaccination status
}
cov_num_names <- cov_num_names[! cov_num_names %in% "cov_num_age"] # remove age
cov_names <- c(cov_factor_names, cov_num_names)
for(i in 1:length(cov_names)){
  surv_formula_age_spl_sex_adjusted <- paste0(surv_formula_age_spl_sex,"+", cov_names[i])
  fit_cox_model <- fit_cox_model_splines <-rms::cph(formula= as.formula(surv_formula_age_spl_sex_adjusted),
                                   data= input, weight=input$weight,surv = TRUE,x=TRUE,y=TRUE)
  which_model = cov_names[i]
  output_file = paste0("output/review/model/HR_age_sex_adjusted_", which_model, "_", analysis)
  results <- cox_output2(fit_cox_model, which_model, output_file, save_output=FALSE)
}
[...]input_select <- input_select%>% rowwise() %>% mutate(fup_end_date=min(out_first_long_covid_date, 
                                                                        death_date,

input_select$lcovid_surv<- as.numeric(input_select$fup_end_date - input_select$index_date)+1
input_select <- input_select %>% mutate(lcovid_cens = ifelse((out_first_long_covid_date <= fup_end_date & 
                                                                  out_first_long_covid_date >= index_date &
                                                                  !is.na(out_first_long_covid_date)), 1, 0))

The code implements Surv(lcovid_surv, lcovid_cens) with cov_cat_sex and rcs(cov_num_age) and adds each covariate in turn, matching the paper’s variable roles.

)[...]ratio_non_cases_to_cases = 20 # this is used in sampling non-cases to increase efficiency without loss of information[...]non_case_inverse_weight=(nrow(input)-nrow(cases))/nrow(non_cases)
input$weight <- ifelse(input$patient_id %in% noncase_ids,
                                    non_case_inverse_weight, 1)[...]robust_fit_cox_model=rms::robcov(fit_cox_model, cluster = input$practice_id)[...]levels(input$cov_cat_imd)[levels(input$cov_cat_imd)==0] <-"0 (missing)"[...]mutate(cov_cat_smoking_status = replace_na(cov_cat_smoking_status, "Missing"))

brief_description: Age- and sex-adjusted Cox proportional hazards model in the primary cohort estimating hazard ratios for demographic predictors of time to first coded long COVID.
paper_text: “We included a missing category for ethnicity, smoking status and IMD.”[...]“Where categorised, age groups were: 18-39, 40-59, 60-79, 80-105 years.”[...]“Hazard ratios by age group (40 to 59, 60 to 79 and 80 to 105 years compared with 18 to 39 years(reference)), were estimated from models including age as a categorical variable, instead of a cubic spline.”
paper_location: “We included a missing category for ethnicity, smoking status and IMD.”
code_text:
levels(input$cov_cat_imd)[levels(input$cov_cat_imd)==0] <-"0 (missing)"
input <- input %>% mutate(cov_cat_smoking_status = recode(cov_cat_smoking_status, "N" = "Never smoker",
                                                            "S" = "Current smoker",
                                                            "E" = "Ever smoker",
                                                            "M" = "Missing"))
input$cov_cat_smoking_status <- ordered(input$cov_cat_smoking_status, levels = c("Never smoker","Ever smoker","Current smoker","Missing"))
input_factor_vars$cov_cat_ethnicity <- relevel(input_factor_vars$cov_cat_ethnicity, ref = "White")
input$cov_cat_sex <- relevel(input$cov_cat_sex, ref = "M")
input$cov_cat_age_group <- ""
input$cov_cat_age_group <- ifelse(input$cov_num_age>=18 & input$cov_num_age<=39, "18_39", input$cov_cat_age_group)
input$cov_cat_age_group <- ifelse(input$cov_num_age>=40 & input$cov_num_age<=59, "40_59", input$cov_cat_age_group)
input$cov_cat_age_group <- ifelse(input$cov_num_age>=60 & input$cov_num_age<=79, "60_79", input$cov_cat_age_group)
input$cov_cat_age_group <- ifelse(input$cov_num_age>=80, "80_105", input$cov_cat_age_group)
input$cov_cat_age_group <- factor(input$cov_cat_age_group, ordered = TRUE)
input_factor_vars <- input_factor_vars %>% mutate(cov_cat_smoking_status = as.character(cov_cat_smoking_status)) %>%
    mutate(cov_cat_smoking_status = replace_na(cov_cat_smoking_status, "Missing")) %>%
    mutate(cov_cat_smoking_status = as.factor(cov_cat_smoking_status))
explanation: The paper specifies missing categories for ethnicity, smoking, and IMD and defines age groups with 18–39 as the reference; the code implements these by creating explicit “Missing” levels, ordering IMD with a “0 (missing)” level, and coding age groups 18_39/40_59/60_79/80_105 as an ordered factor.

brief_description: Multivariable Cox proportional hazards model in the primary cohort fully adjusting for demographic variables (and, per methods, all patient characteristics) to estimate hazard ratios for time to first coded long COVID.
paper_text: “The outcome was clinically coded long COVID, constructed from the date of the first record of any of the 15 UK SNOMED-CT codes for long COVID 6 in English primary care records, consisting of two diagnostic codes, three referral codes and 10 assessment codes (Supplement, Table S2). Time to the outcome event was defined as days from participant specific follow-up start date (Supplement, Table S1).” [...] “Demographic variables included age, sex, obesity, ethnicity, region and deprivation.” [...] “Patient characteristics included demographic variables, and health behavioural and clinical factors that may be associated with coded long COVID 6 7 , and the frequency of GP-patient interactions, which could be an indicator of patient access to care and ability to interact with GP. [...] History of the disease diagnoses, chosen based on previous literature on risk factors for long COVID 7 and defined on or before the cohort and participant specific follow-up start date, was coded as separate indicator variables: asthma, cancer, chronic cardiac disease, chronic kidney disease, chronic liver disease, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, chronic respiratory disease, dementia, diabetes, dysplenia (dysfunctional-spleen), haematological cancer, heart failure, hypertension, mental health condition, organ transplant, other immunosuppressive condition, other neurological condition, post-viral fatigue, psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis, systematic lupus erythematosus (SLE), and stroke.” [...] “In each cohort, hazard ratios with 95% confidence intervals (CI) for each patient characteristic were estimated from age-and-sex adjusted Cox proportional hazards (PH) models, and then all patient characteristics were included in a multivariable Cox PH model.”
paper_location: “The outcome was clinically coded long COVID, constructed from the date of the first record of any of the 15 UK SNOMED-CT codes for long COVID 6 in English primary care records, consisting of two diagnostic codes, three referral codes and 10 assessment codes (Supplement, Table S2). Time to the outcome event was defined as days from participant specific follow-up start date (Supplement, Table S1).”
code_text:
covariate_names <- names(input)[grep("cov_", names(input))]

covariate_names <- covariate_names[-grep("age", covariate_names)]

surv_formula <- paste0(
  "Surv(lcovid_surv, lcovid_cens) ~ ",
  paste(covariate_names, collapse = "+"),
  "+rms::rcs(cov_num_age,parms=knot_placement)"
)
[...] 
fit_cox_model_splines <-rms::cph(formula= as.formula(surv_formula),
                                 data= input, weight=input$weight,surv = TRUE,x=TRUE,y=TRUE)
explanation: The paper specifies the outcome and that all patient characteristics (demographics, behaviours, and listed comorbidities) are included in a multivariable Cox model. The code builds a Cox model with Surv(lcovid_surv, lcovid_cens) and includes all covariates prefixed with “cov_” (i.e., all patient characteristics) plus age as a spline.

explanation: The code sets the same 1:20 non-case sampling ratio, computes inverse-probability weights, and uses robust standard errors via a robust covariance estimator, matching the paper’s specified parameters. These implement the stated sampling and estimation approach.

  input <- input %>% filter(!is.na(out_covid_date) &out_covid_date>= index_date & out_covid_date <= cohort_end_date)

}
[...] input_select <- input_select%>% rowwise() %>% mutate(fup_end_date=min(out_first_long_covid_date,

input_select$lcovid_surv<- as.numeric(input_select$fup_end_date - input_select$index_date)+1

[...] input$sub_cat_covid_phenotype <- "no_infection"
